title: Cloud Run Functions
aim: To create and deploy 2nd generation Cloud Run functions triggered by HTTP and Cloud Storage.

steps.description: Open Cloud Shell and enable the necessary services for Cloud Functions and Eventarc.

steps.command: gcloud services enable artifactregistry.googleapis.com cloudfunctions.googleapis.com cloudbuild.googleapis.com eventarc.googleapis.com run.googleapis.com logging.googleapis.com pubsub.googleapis.com cloudaicompanion.googleapis.com

tasks.title: Create and Deploy an HTTP Function

steps.description: Create a directory for the function code.
Create an 'index.js' file with the function logic.
Deploy the function to Cloud Run (2nd Gen).
steps.command: gcloud functions deploy nodejs-http-function --gen2 --runtime nodejs22 --entry-point helloWorld --trigger-http --region [REGION] --timeout 600s --max-instances 1

steps.description: Verify the deployment by visiting the generated Trigger URL.

tasks.title: Create a Cloud Storage Triggered Function

steps.description: Create a globally unique Cloud Storage bucket.

steps.command: gcloud functions deploy nodejs-storage-function --gen2 --runtime nodejs22 --entry-point helloStorage --trigger-bucket gs://gcf-gen2-storage-[PROJECT_ID] --region [REGION]

steps.description: Test by uploading a file to the bucket and checking logs.
steps.command:
echo "Hello World" > test.txt
gsutil cp test.txt gs://gcf-gen2-storage-[PROJECT_ID]

viva.question: What is Cloud Run Functions?
viva.answer: A serverless execution environment for building and connecting cloud services.

viva.question: Difference between 1st and 2nd gen?
viva.answer: 2nd gen is built on top of Cloud Run and Eventarc, offering longer timeouts and better performance.

result: Successfully deployed HTTP and Storage triggered serverless functions.
conclusion: Understood the event-driven architecture of Cloud Functions.
